title: Worked Example - Swap Using Pass By Reference

content.problemStatement:
This example demonstrates swapping two integers using pass by reference in C.
Normally, functions receive copies of variables (pass by value), so changes don’t affect originals.
By passing addresses (pointers), the function accesses and modifies the original variables directly.

**When executed, the code will show:**
```default
Before swap: x = 5, y = 10
After swap: x = 10, y = 5
```
